India Blockchain Tour · Annual Recognition

Person of the Year
in Web3

2025

Chosen by the Web3 community. Announced at India Blockchain Tour.

Octaloop Person of the Year in Web3 · 2025
Donald Trump
45th & 47th President of the United States — who made crypto a cornerstone of American economic policy

"In 2025, no single person did more to legitimise crypto on the world stage than the man in the White House."

— Octaloop Editorial

Every year at the final city of India Blockchain Tour, we ask our community a direct question: who moved the needle the most for Web3 this year? Not the most popular. Not the most followed. The most consequential. The answers from thousands of attendees, founders, traders, and builders across India's largest Web3 event series shape a shortlist — and from that shortlist, a winner emerges.

2025 was not a quiet year. Bitcoin crossed milestones that once seemed unreachable. Regulatory clarity came — slowly, messily, but it came. India's crypto industry entered its most mature phase yet. And at the centre of the global story, one figure stood apart from the rest: a politician who turned "pro-crypto" from a campaign promise into a governing reality.

Donald Trump's return to the White House did not just change American crypto policy. It changed the entire global conversation about whether crypto deserved a seat at the table. That conversation is now settled. It does.

The Process

How the winner is chosen

The Octaloop Person of the Year in Web3 is not an editorial award given behind closed doors. It is a community mandate — earned through the votes of thousands of people who live and breathe this industry every day.

01

Octaloop Nominates

Our team curates a shortlist of individuals who shaped Web3 in a meaningful way that year — across policy, technology, markets, and culture. Nominees span the globe but are judged on their impact on the broader ecosystem.

02

Community Votes at IBT

At the final city of India Blockchain Tour — our flagship multi-city Web3 event series — the entire audience votes live. These are founders, investors, traders, developers, and builders. Their voice is the verdict.

03

The Winner is Announced

The results are tallied on stage and the winner is announced to the room — no editors, no panels, no politics. The community decides. We just ask the question.

The Shortlist

The 2025 Nominees

Eight names. One year. Each of these individuals left a mark on Web3 in 2025 that the community recognised and debated. Here's why they were on the list.

Donald Trump
47th President, USA

Signed executive orders protecting self-custody, established a Bitcoin strategic reserve, and turned the US into the most crypto-friendly major economy on earth.
Nirmala Sitharaman
Finance Minister, India

India's most powerful economic voice continued to shape the country's evolving stance on digital assets — walking the tightrope between regulation and growth.
Michael Saylor
Executive Chairman, MicroStrategy

Accumulated Bitcoin at a scale that no institution had attempted before, turning MicroStrategy into a proxy for Bitcoin and making corporate treasury adoption mainstream.
CZ
Founder, Binance

Served his sentence, walked free, and remained the most-watched figure in crypto — proof that in this industry, no one truly exits the stage.
Brian Armstrong
CEO, Coinbase

Fought the SEC in court and won. Coinbase's legal victory against the regulator marked a turning point for how crypto companies would defend themselves in America.
Vitalik Buterin
Co-founder, Ethereum

Continued to push Ethereum's technical roadmap forward — The Purge, The Splurge — keeping the network at the centre of global developer attention.
Sumit Gupta
CEO, CoinDCX

Built and scaled India's largest crypto platform through one of the most difficult regulatory environments any exchange has faced, and kept the lights on for millions of Indian users.
Nishchal Shetty
CEO, WazirX

Navigated the aftermath of one of India's largest crypto exchange hacks with transparency and tenacity — a case study in crisis leadership in a trust-dependent industry.
And the winner is

Donald Trump

Octaloop Web3 Person of the Year · 2025

When Donald Trump won the US presidential election in November 2024, the crypto industry celebrated — cautiously. Campaign promises are easy. Governing is harder. But by the time 2025 was done, Trump had delivered in a way that even his supporters hadn't fully anticipated.

The executive orders came early and moved fast. Crypto mining protections. A task force on digital asset policy. And then the headline that reverberated across every trading floor, Discord server, and conference panel on earth: a Bitcoin Strategic Reserve. The United States government, holding Bitcoin as a national asset. The idea that had once been dismissed as fringe thinking became official policy.

Beyond the policy, what Trump did was change the cultural weather around crypto. In Washington, being pro-Bitcoin went from a liability to a signal of economic seriousness. Other countries watched. Some followed. The Overton window on crypto — once defined by suspicion and restriction — shifted permanently.

The IBT community voted for Trump not because of party or politics, but because of impact. In 2025, no single person did more to move the global needle on crypto legitimacy. That's the only metric that matters for this award.

Also see: Person of the Year 2024 — Elon Musk
